subject: Disabling http ports on DMaaP
commit_message:
Disabling http ports on DMaaP

Change-Id: I8cf5a6ac58d38c6e5c818259baf7d69615eb9803
Signed-off-by: efiacor <fiachra.corcoran@est.tech>
Issue-ID: DMAAP-1400

entries.message:

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/3836/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/oom-master-verify-helm/3836

Patch Set 1:

Having reviewed a lot of code recently, most if not all the internal components are relying on "http" port of DMaaP...
What I can propose is to use the new (and soon to be merged ;)) service templates on message router (I actually need a "volunteer" to validate) which allow https only on outside and https+http on inside?

If it's fine for you, I can make the review (knowing I'll have to change the sts in order to be compatible with this new template).

What's your thoughts?

Patch Set 1:

> Having reviewed a lot of code recently, most if not all the
 > internal components are relying on "http" port of DMaaP...
 > What I can propose is to use the new (and soon to be merged ;))
 > service templates on message router (I actually need a "volunteer"
 > to validate) which allow https only on outside and https+http on
 > inside?
 > 
 > If it's fine for you, I can make the review (knowing I'll have to
 > change the sts in order to be compatible with this new template).
 > 
 > What's your thoughts?

Hi Sylvain,

Not sure I follow. This patch doesn't impact DMaaP Message Router. Only DMaaP DataRouter and DMaaP BusController.

We excluded Message Router because of most/all clients using http.

Patch Set 1:

> > Having reviewed a lot of code recently, most if not all the
 > > internal components are relying on "http" port of DMaaP...
 > > What I can propose is to use the new (and soon to be merged ;))
 > > service templates on message router (I actually need a
 > "volunteer"
 > > to validate) which allow https only on outside and https+http on
 > > inside?
 > >
 > > If it's fine for you, I can make the review (knowing I'll have to
 > > change the sts in order to be compatible with this new template).
 > >
 > > What's your thoughts?
 > 
 > Hi Sylvain,
 > 
 > Not sure I follow. This patch doesn't impact DMaaP Message Router.
 > Only DMaaP DataRouter and DMaaP BusController.
 > 
 > We excluded Message Router because of most/all clients using http.

The reason the DataRouter healthcheck failed is because of Robot dependency patch here - 
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/testsuite/oom/+/103055/

Patch Set 1:

Hi @Fiachra,
I misread your patch proposal. 
I thought it was only for message router sorry :-/

Are there "internal" users of BC and DR? If yes, does they already use https?

Patch Set 1:

> Hi @Fiachra,
 > I misread your patch proposal.
 > I thought it was only for message router sorry :-/
 > 
 > Are there "internal" users of BC and DR? If yes, does they already
 > use https?

No problem. There are a few clients but I'm pretty sure they have moved to https.

DR -> BC
DCAE -> BC
DFC -> DR
BC -> DR

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+2

OK, pods and HC of DMaaP are in good shape so I believe we can merge as soon as Dominic has given its +1
Change has been successfully merged by Krzysztof Opasiak
